summary: Possible name-squat of @livekit/agents with placeholder contents
details: Unscoped npm package `livekit-agents` resembles LiveKit's official scoped package `@livekit/agents`. The tarball ships a trivial stub exporting only `greet(name)` and a README titled `# prek` with a generic description (`A modern npm package`) that does not match the package name. No declared author, no lifecycle scripts, and no install-time or import-time behavior that would harm an installer (no exfiltration, no dropper, no silent relay, no credential access). The name-similarity to a well-known LiveKit package combined with mismatched placeholder contents is consistent with a name-squat / staging publish, but this version contains no installer-harm payload. Name-similarity judgment is subjective and warrants human review rather than an automated block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
